raw.name: Heavy Barding
entries: You can purchase special armor for animals, called barding. Combat-trained animals are trained in heavy barding. Barding uses the same rules as armor except for the following. The Price and Bulk of barding depend on the animal's size (use statistics for Small barding for creatures below Small size). Barding can't be etched with magic runes, though special barding might be available.

raw.name: Light Barding
entries: You can purchase special armor for animals, called barding. All animals have a trained proficiency rank in light barding. Barding uses the same rules as armor except for the following. The Price and Bulk of barding depend on the animal's size (use statistics for Small barding for creatures below Small size). Barding can't be etched with magic runes, though special barding might be available.

raw.name: Acid Flask
entries: This flask filled with corrosive acid deals 1 acid damage, the listed {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage}, and the listed acid {@trait splash} damage. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.
It deals {@damage 1d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 1 acid {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: {@damage 1d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 1 acid {@trait splash} damage.

entries: You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 2d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 2 acid {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: {@damage 2d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 2 acid {@trait splash} damage. You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries: You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 3d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 3 acid {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: {@damage 3d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 3 acid {@trait splash} damage. You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries: You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 4d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 4 acid {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: {@damage 4d6} {@condition persistent damage||persistent acid damage} and 4 acid {@trait splash} damage. You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries.replace: the listed {@condition persistent damage\|\|persistent acid damage}, and the listed acid {@trait splash} damage\. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls\.

raw.name: Adamantine
entries: Mined from rocks that fell from the heavens, adamantine is one of the hardest metals known. It has a shiny, black appearance, and it is prized for its amazing resiliency and ability to hold an incredibly sharp edge.

raw.name: Adamantine Armor
entries: Adamantine armor has a shiny, black appearance and is amazingly durable.

craftReq: The initial raw materials must include adamantine worth at least 200 gp + 20 gp per Bulk.
The initial raw materials must include adamantine worth at least 16,000 gp + 1,600 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Adamantine Shield
entries: Adamantine shields are particularly sturdy, and when used for a shield bash, they are adamantine weapons.

variants.name: Standard-Grade Adamantine Buckler
craftReq: adamantine worth at least 50 gp
adamantine worth at least 55 gp

variants.name: High-Grade Adamantine Buckler
craftReq: adamantine worth at least 4,000 gp
adamantine worth at least 4,400 gp

raw.name: Adamantine Weapon
entries: Adamantine weapons have a shiny black appearance and cut through lesser items with ease. They treat any object they hit as if it had half as much Hardness as usual, unless the object's Hardness is greater than that of the adamantine weapon.

craftReq: at least 175 gp of adamantine + 17.5 gp per Bulk.
at least 6,750 gp of adamantine + 675 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Adventurer's Pack
entries: This item is the starter kit for an adventurer, containing the essential items for exploration and survival. The Bulk value is for the entire pack together, but see the descriptions of individual items as necessary. The pack contains the following items: a {@item backpack}, a {@item bedroll}, {@item chalk (10)||10 pieces of chalk}, {@item flint and steel}, {@item rope (50 feet)||50 feet of rope}, 2 weeks' {@item Rations (1 week)||rations}, {@item soap}, 5 {@item torch||torches}, and a {@item waterskin}.

raw.name: Aeon Stone
entries: Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body.

You can stow an aeon stone with an {@action Interact} action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful {@action Disarm} action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.

There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different shape, color, and {@trait magical} effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a {@item wayfinder|crb}.

variants.name: Clear Spindle Aeon Stone
entries: You don't need to eat or drink while this aeon stone is invested by you. This aeon stone doesn't function until it has been worn continuously for a week and invested each day therein. If it's invested by someone else, this interval starts over.
The resonant power allows you to cast {@spell air bubble} as a primal innate spell once per day.

variants.name: Dull Gray Aeon Stone
entries: A dull gray aeon stone has lost its special magical properties, sometimes as a result of overusing a tourmaline sphere or pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone. It still orbits your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hands-free option for various spells that target an object, like continual flame.
Dull gray aeon stones have no resonant power.

variants.name: Gold Nodule Aeon Stone
entries: When a gold nodule aeon stone is created, its creator chooses a language they know to store within the crystal. When you invest the stone, you gain the ability to understand, speak, and write that language.
The resonant power allows you to cast {@spell comprehend language} as an occult innate spell once per day.

variants.name: Lavender And Green Ellipsoid Aeon Stone
entries: This functions as a {@item pale lavender ellipsoid aeon stone|CRB}, but it casts an 8th-level {@spell dispel magic} spell with a counteract modifier of +31.
The resonant power allows you to cast {@spell detect magic} and read aura as arcane innate spells at will.

variants.name: Orange Prism Aeon Stone
entries: An orange prism aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Occultism}, or {@skill Religion} checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone.

{@b Activate} {@as 1} envision; {@b Effect} If your next action is to {@action Cast a Spell}, that spell's level is 1 higher (maximum 10th level) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted.

variants.name: Pale Lavender Ellipsoid Aeon Stone
entries: This aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power allows you to cast the {@spell read aura} cantrip as an arcane innate spell.

{@b Activate} {@as R} envision; {@b Frequency} once per day; {@b Trigger} A spell targets you; {@b Effect} The stone casts a 6th-level {@spell dispel magic} spell in an attempt to counteract the triggering spell, with a counteract modifier of +22. This can be used only on spells that specifically target you—not area spells that don't have targets. If it succeeds, it counteracts the spell for all targets if other creatures were targeted in addition to you.
Each time you activate this aeon stone, attempt a DC {@flatDC 5} flat check. On a failure, the stone permanently turns into a {@item dull gray aeon stone|crb}.

variants.name: Pink Rhomboid Aeon Stone
entries: When you invest this stone, you gain 15 temporary Hit Points. If the stone's effects are suppressed, you lose any of the temporary Hit Points remaining until it returns. The temporary Hit Points refresh during your daily preparations; they do not refresh if you re-invest the stone, or invest another pink rhomboid aeon stone, before then.
The resonant power allows you to cast the {@spell stabilize} cantrip as a divine innate spell.

variants.name: Tourmaline Sphere Aeon Stone
entries: When you would die from the {@condition dying} condition (typically at {@condition dying 4}), this aeon stone automatically activates and reduces your {@condition dying} value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to {@condition dying 3}). The stone then permanently turns into a {@item dull gray aeon stone|crb}. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-level {@spell heal} as a divine innate spell once per day.

variants.name: Agate Ellipsoid Aeon Stone
entries: This aeon stone allows you to cast 2nd-level {@spell augury} as a divine innate spell once per day.
The resonant power causes the {@spell augury} spell from the aeon stone to always succeed at the DC {@flatDC 6} flat check to give an answer other than "nothing."

variants.name: Azure Briolette Aeon Stone
entries: This aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit.

{@b Activate} {@as R} envision; {@b Trigger} An enemy fails its save against a {@trait mental} spell you cast using one of your spell slots; {@b Effect} You gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's level until your next turn.
The resonant power grants a separate activation.
{@b Activate} {@as 2} command, {@action Interact}; {@b Frequency} once per day; {@b Effect} You gain the effect of a {@spell chill touch} arcane cantrip.

variants.name: Dusty Rose Prism Aeon Stone
entries: This aeon stone allows you to cast the 1st-level {@spell shield} cantrip as an arcane innate spell, surrounding yourself in pink energy.
The resonant power increases the damage prevented by your aeon stone's {@spell shield} spell from 5 to 10.

variants.name: Pale Orange Rhomboid Aeon Stone
entries: The first time each day you die, your vital and spiritual essences mingle together and reside within this aeon stone.

You have a limited sense of how many life forces are currently within 30 feet of the stone, which you can potentially use to determine when it's safe to call on the stone's power. At any time within the next hour after your body died, you can spend 1 minute concentrating in order to return to your body.

As long as your body is still intact, you are no longer dead—you are restored to 1 HP with the {@condition doomed 3} and {@condition wounded 3} conditions. You can benefit from the effects of only one pale orange rhomboid aeon stone in a given day.

The resonant power allows you to wait more than an hour to return to your body with the aeon stone; as long as the wayfinder remains on the body, your body doesn't decay and you can return at any point.

variants.name: Pearlescent Pyramid Aeon Stone
entries: While invested, this aeon stone grants the benefit of the {@item ghost touch} property rune to your weapons and unarmed attacks.
The resonant power grants a special activation.
{@b Activate} {@as 1} envision; {@b Frequency} once per minute; {@b Effect} You gain the effects of see invisibility for 1 round.

variants.name: Pearly White Spindle Aeon Stone
entries: When you invest this aeon stone, it slowly starts healing your wounds, restoring 1 HP every minute.
The resonant power grants you resistance 1 to negative damage.

variants.name: Western Star Aeon Stone
entries: This aeon stone must be activated to provide a benefit.
{@b Activate} {@as 2} envision, {@action Interact}; {@b Effect} You activate the aeon stone to gain the effects of a 1st-level {@spell illusory disguise}.
The resonant power allows you to render all of your aeon stones and your wayfinder {@condition invisible} whenever you use the activation to gain the effects of illusory disguise.

variants.name: Black Pearl Aeon Stone
entries: This black pearl sparkles with light.

{@b Activate} {@as R} envision; {@b Trigger} you are targeted by a {@trait mental} effect. {@b Effect} You receive a +4 status bonus to your saving throw against the triggering effect. Whether or not your save is successful, the aeon stone attempts a counteract check at +30 to immediately reflect a copy of the effect back at the originator, targeting it using the creature's own relevant statistics but controlling the effect as if you had cast it. Every time you activate this aeon stone, attempt a DC {@flatDC 5} flat check. On a failure, the stone shatters and loses its magical properties forever.
The resonant power allows you to cast {@spell sending} once per day.

raw.name: Alarm Snare
entries: You create an alarm snare by rigging one or more noisy objects to a trip wire or pressure plate. When you create an alarm snare, you designate a range between 100 to 500 feet at which it can be heard. When a Small or larger creature enters the square, the snare makes a noise loud enough that it can be heard by all creatures in the range you designated.

raw.name: Alchemist Goggles
entries: These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses. While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Crafting} checks to {@action Craft} alchemical items and a +1 item bonus to attack rolls with alchemical bombs. You can also ignore lesser cover when making {@action Strike||Strikes} with alchemical bombs.
The item bonuses are both +2.
The item bonuses are both +3.

raw.name: Alchemist's Fire
entries: Alchemist's fire is a combination of volatile liquids that ignite when exposed to air. Alchemist's fire deals the listed fire damage, {@condition persistent damage||persistent fire damage}, and {@trait splash} damage. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.
The bomb deals {@damage 1d8} fire damage, 1 {@condition persistent damage||persistent fire damage}, and 1 fire {@trait splash} damage.
You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 2d8} fire damage, 2 {@condition persistent damage||persistent fire damage}, and 2 fire {@trait splash} damage.
You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 3d8} fire damage, 3 {@condition persistent damage||persistent fire damage}, and 3 fire {@trait splash} damage.
You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 4d8} fire damage, 4 {@condition persistent damage||persistent fire damage}, and 4 fire {@trait splash} damage.

items: Alchemist's fire is a combination of volatile liquids that ignite when exposed to air.

raw.name: Alchemist's Lab
entries: You need an alchemist's lab to {@action Craft} alchemical items during downtime.

variants.name: Expanded Alchemist's Lab
entries: An expanded alchemist's lab gives a +1 item bonus to {@skill Crafting} checks to create alchemical items.

raw.name: Alchemist's Tools
entries: This mobile collection of vials and chemicals can be used for simple alchemical tasks. If you wear your alchemist's tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.
{@note *You can use the set of tools with one hand if you are wearing it.}

raw.name: Aligned Oil
entries: This oil fills a weapon with cosmic power of an alignment. Each aligned oil is crafted to one alignment: chaos, evil, good, or law (such as a good-aligned oil). A weapon anointed with this oil gains the effects of the property rune matching its alignment: anarchic (chaos), axiomatic (law), holy (good), or unholy (evil)
This lasts for 1 minute.

raw.name: Anarchic
raw.usage: etched onto a weapon without an axiomatic rune
entries: An anarchic rune is jagged and asymmetrical, channeling chaotic energy. A weapon with this rune deals an additional {@damage 1d6} chaotic damage against lawful targets. If you are lawful, you are {@condition enfeebled 2} while carrying or wielding this weapon.

When you critically succeed at a {@action Strike} with this weapon against a lawful creature, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 1 or 2, you deal double minimum damage; on a 3 or 4, double your damage normally; on a 5 or 6, you deal double maximum damage.
craftReq: You are chaotic.

raw.name: Animal Staff
entries: This staff is topped with carved animal and monster heads.
While wielding the staff, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to {@skill Nature} checks to identify animals.
You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.

craftReq: Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.

items: {@bold 2nd} {@spell animal messenger}, {@spell speak with animals}, {@spell summon animal}
{@bold 5th} {@spell animal form}, {@spell moon frenzy}, {@spell summon animal}

raw.name: Anklets of Alacrity
entries: These gem-studded golden anklets give you a +3 item bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks. When you invest the anklets, you either increase your Dexterity score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.
You click the anklets together, gaining a +20-foot status bonus to all your Speeds and the effects of water walk for the next 10 minutes.

raw.name: Antidote
entries: An antidote protects you against toxins. Upon drinking an antidote, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against poisons for 6 hours.
You gain a +2 item bonus.

entries.with: a +2 item bonus

entries: You gain a +3 item bonus.

entries.with: a +3 item bonus

entries: You gain a +4 item bonus.

entries.with: a +4 item bonus

entries: You gain a +4 item bonus, and when you drink the antidote, you can immediately attempt a saving throw against one poison of 14th level or lower affecting you. If you succeed, the poison is neutralized.

entries.with: a +4 item bonus

items: When you drink the antidote, you can immediately attempt a saving throw against one poison of 14th level or lower affecting you. If you succeed, the poison is neutralized.

raw.name: Antimagic
entries: This intricate rune displaces spell energy, granting you a +1 status bonus to saving throws against {@trait magical} effects.

entries.trigger: A spell targets you or includes you in its area
entries: The armor attempts to counteract the triggering spell with the effect of a 7th-level {@spell dispel magic} spell and a counteract modifier of +26.

craftReq: Supply one casting of dispel magic.

raw.name: Antimagic Oil
entries: This oil contains energy that repels nearly all types of magic.

When you apply this oil to armor, the creature wearing the armor becomes immune to all spells, effects of magic items (the wearer's and those of others), and effects with the {@trait magical} trait for 1 minute. The oil affects neither the magic of the armor nor the fundamental runes of weapons attacking the wearer.
Magical effects from a source of 20th level or higher, such as a deity, still function on the armor's wearer.

raw.name: Antiplague
entries: Antiplague can fortify the body's defenses against diseases.
Upon drinking an antiplague, you gain an item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases for 24 hours; this applies to your daily save against a disease's progression.
You gain a +2 item bonus.

entries.with: a +2 item bonus

entries: You gain a +3 item bonus.

entries.with: a +3 item bonus

entries: You gain a +4 item bonus.

entries.with: a +4 item bonus

entries: You gain a +4 item bonus, and when you drink the antiplague, you can immediately attempt a saving throw against one disease of 14th level or lower affecting you. If you succeed, you are cured of the disease.

entries.with: a +4 item bonus

items: When you drink the antiplague, you can immediately attempt a saving throw against one disease of 14th level or lower affecting you. If you succeed, you are cured of the disease.

raw.name: Armbands of Athleticism
entries: Skilled awl work has imprinted images of a muscled weightlifter into these tiered leather bands, which grant you enhanced stamina and skill when performing athletic exercises. While fastened to your upper arms, the armbands give you a +2 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks. In addition, whenever you use an action to {@action Climb} or {@action Swim} and you succeed at the {@skill Athletics} check, add a +5-foot item bonus to the distance you move.
The bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks is +3, and the bonus to a successful check to {@action Climb} or {@action Swim} is +10 feet.

raw.name: Armor Potency
entries: Magic wards deflect attacks. Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune.

You can upgrade the armor potency rune already etched on a suit of armor to a stronger version, increasing the values of the existing rune to those of the new rune. You must have the formula of the stronger rune to do so, and the Price of the upgrade is the difference between the two runes' Prices.

craftReq: You are an expert in {@skill Crafting}.

entries.with: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune.

craftReq: You are a master in {@skill Crafting}.
entries: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 2, and the armor can be etched with two property runes.

entries.with: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 2, and the armor can be etched with two property runes.

craftReq: You are legendary in {@skill Crafting}.
entries: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 3, and the armor can be etched with three property runes.

entries.with: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 3, and the armor can be etched with three property runes.

entries.replace: Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune\.

raw.name: Arrow
entries: These projectiles are the ammunition for bows. The shaft of an arrow is made of wood. It is stabilized in flight by fletching at one end and bears a metal head on the other.
{@note Different weapons have different prices for ammunition. The prices listed are thus, only for reference in the weapon statblocks themselves.}

raw.name: Arrow-catching Shield
entries: This remarkable wooden shield (Hardness 10, HP 60, BT 30) is carved with images of overlapping fletched arrows.

entries.trigger: A ranged weapon {@action Strike} targets a creature within 15 feet of you when you have this shield raised, and the attacker has not yet rolled their attack
entries: The triggering {@action Strike} targets you instead of its normal target.
If it hits, you gain the effects of the {@feat Shield Block} reaction.

raw.name: Arsenic
entries: This toxin is a compound of arsenic and other substances. You can't reduce your {@condition sickened} condition while affected.

raw.name: Artisan's Tools
entries: You need these tools to create items from raw materials with the {@action Craft} skill. Different sets are needed for different work, as determined by the GM; for example, blacksmith's tools differ from woodworker's tools. If you wear your artisan's tools, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.
{@note *You can use the set of tools with one hand if you are wearing it.}

variants.name: Sterling Artisan's Tools
entries: Sterling artisan's tools give you a +1 item bonus to the check.

raw.name: Axiomatic
raw.usage: etched onto a weapon without an anarchic rune
entries: Complex and symmetrical, an axiomatic rune imbues a weapon with lawful energy. A weapon with this rune deals an additional {@damage 1d6} lawful damage against chaotic targets. If you are chaotic, you are {@condition enfeebled 2} while carrying or wielding this weapon.

When you critically succeed at an attack roll with this weapon against a chaotic creature, instead of rolling, count each weapon damage die as average damage rounded up (3 for d4, 4 for d6, 5 for d8, 6 for d10, 7 for d12).
craftReq: You are lawful.

raw.name: Backpack
entries: A backpack holds up to 4 Bulk of items. and the first 2 Bulk of these items don't count against your Bulk limits. If you're carrying or stowing the pack rather than wearing it on your back, its Bulk is light instead of negligible.

raw.name: Bag of Holding
entries: Though it appears to be a cloth sack decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a bag of holding opens into an extradimensional space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the bag of holding itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type.

You can {@action Interact} with the bag of holding to put items in or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside.

If the bag is overloaded or {@condition broken}, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to {@action Escape} against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane.

variants.name: Bag of Holding (Type I)
entries: Capacity 25 Bulk.

entries.with: The bag's extradimensional space can hold 25 Bulk.

variants.name: Bag of Holding (Type II)
entries: Capacity 50 Bulk.

entries.with: The bag's extradimensional space can hold 50 Bulk.

variants.name: Bag of Holding (Type III)
entries: Capacity 100 Bulk.

entries.with: The bag's extradimensional space can hold 100 Bulk.

variants.name: Bag of Holding (Type IV)
entries: Capacity 150 Bulk.

entries.with: The bag's extradimensional space can hold 150 Bulk.

entries.replace: The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type\.

raw.name: Barding of the Zephyr
entries: This {@item light barding (generic)||light barding} is covered in stylized wind motifs. When you suit up your animal companion, the barding adjusts to fit your animal companion regardless of its shape.
When your companion falls, wind picks it up from below; it gains the effects of feather fall.

You trace a finger along the wind motifs on the barding, granting your companion wearing the barding a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes. Even if the companion doesn't have the mount special ability, it can still {@action Fly} while being ridden.

raw.name: Barkskin Potion
entries: After you drink this bitter draft, your skin thickens like bark. You gain the effects of a 2nd-level {@spell barkskin} spell for 10 minutes.

raw.name: Basic Crafter's Book
entries: This book contains the {@quickref formulas|CRB|1|formulas|0} for {@action Craft||Crafting} {@filter all 0-level common items in Chapter 6: Equipment of the Core Rulebook|items||Source=CRB|Rarity=Common|Type=Equipment;!Specific Variant|Level=[0]}.

raw.name: Beacon Shot
entries: The shaft of a beacon shot is studded with tiny flecks of glimmering gemstones. When an activated beacon shot hits a target, it embeds itself into that target and spews sparks for 1 minute. If the target is {@condition invisible}, it becomes merely {@condition hidden} to creatures who would otherwise be unable to see it. The sparks also negate the {@condition concealed} condition if the target was otherwise {@condition concealed}.
A creature can remove the arrow or bolt by using an {@action Interact} basic action and succeeding at a DC 20 {@skill Athletics} check.

raw.name: Belladonna
entries: Sometimes called "deadly nightshade," belladonna is a widely available toxin produced from a plant similar to a tomato.

raw.name: Belt of Giant Strength
entries: This thick leather belt is decorated with a buckle carved from glittering quartz in the shape of a fist. You gain a +3 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks to lift a heavy object, {@action Escape}, and {@action Force Open}. When you invest the belt, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

entries.trigger: You are targeted by a thrown rock attack, or a rock would fall on you
entries: Attempt an {@skill Athletics} check to grab the triggering rock. Use the {@skill Athletics} DC of the creature throwing the rock, the DC of the hazard or other effect, or DC 35 if no other DC is applicable. You must have a free hand to catch the rock, but you can {@action Release} anything you're holding in a hand as part of this reaction.

entries.Success: You safely catch the rock, take no damage, and are now holding the rock.

raw.name: Belt of Regeneration
entries: This belt is crafted from rubbery troll hide that's green and orange in color. You gain 15 temporary Hit Points the first time you invest the belt in a day. When you invest the belt, you either increase your Constitution score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.

You tighten the belt one notch to gain a regeneration effect. For {@dice 2d4} rounds, at the start of your turn each round, you recover 15 Hit Points unless you took acid or fire damage since the start of your previous turn.

raw.name: Belt of the Five Kings
entries: Made from interlocking plates of silver and gold, this heavy belt bears stylized miniature images of five kingly dwarves. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to {@skill Diplomacy} checks to {@action Make an Impression} with dwarves or make a {@action Request} from dwarves. You also gain a +1 circumstance bonus to {@skill Intimidation} checks against giants and orcs.
The belt also grants you {@ability darkvision} and the ability to understand, speak, and write the {@language Dwarven} language.
You tighten the belt one notch to gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level and grant allies within 20 feet of you {@ability darkvision}. Both effects last for 10 minutes.

raw.name: Berserker's Cloak
entries: This bearskin includes the head and bared teeth of the mighty creature from which it was taken. When worn, the cloak drapes over your head and around your shoulders, imbuing you with a bear's ferocity. If you have the {@action Rage} action, while raging you grow jaws that deal {@damage 1d10} piercing damage and claws that deal {@damage 1d6} slashing damage and have the {@trait agile} trait.

This transformation is a {@trait morph} effect, and both the jaws and claws are unarmed attacks in the {@group brawling} weapon group. You gain the benefits of a {@item +1 weapon potency} rune and a {@item striking} rune with these attacks (gaining a +1 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the weapon damage dice by one).

If you have an {@class barbarian||animal instinct|animal} and the bestial rage instinct ability, instead of gaining these unarmed attacks, your unarmed attacks from the bestial rage instinct ability gain the benefits of a {@item +2 weapon potency} rune and a {@item greater striking} rune (granting a +2 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the weapon damage dice by two).
craftReq: You are a barbarian with the {@class barbarian||animal instinct|animal}.

entries: You gain the benefits of a {@item +2 weapon potency} rune and a {@item greater striking} rune, or a {@item +3 weapon potency} rune and a {@item major striking} rune if you have an {@class barbarian||animal instinct|animal} and the bestial rage instinct ability.

entries.with: a {@item major striking}
a {@item greater striking}
three
two

raw.name: Bestial Mutagen
entries: Your features transform into something bestial and you take on muscle mass, but your lumbering form is clumsy.
{@b Benefit} You gain an item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack with the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack.
{@b Drawback} You take a –1 penalty to AC and a –2 penalty to Reflex saves.
You gain a +1 item bonus, your claw deals {@damage 1d4} slashing damage, your jaws deal {@damage 1d6} piercing damage, and the duration is 1 minute.

entries.with: You gain a +1 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d4} slashing damage and has the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d6} piercing damage. The duration is 1 minute.

entries: You gain a +2 item bonus, your claw deals {@damage 1d6} slashing damage, your jaws deal {@damage 1d8} piercing damage, and the duration is 10 minutes.

entries.with: You gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d6} slashing damage and has the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d8} piercing damage. The duration is 10 minutes.

entries: You gain a +3 item bonus, your claw deals {@damage 1d8} slashing damage, your jaws deal {@damage 1d10} piercing damage, and the duration is 1 hour.

entries.with: You gain a +3 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d8} slashing damage and has the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d10} piercing damage. The duration is 1 hour.

entries: You gain a +4 item bonus, your claw deals {@damage 1d8} slashing damage, your jaws deal {@damage 1d10} piercing damage, and the duration is 1 hour. You gain weapon specialization with the claws and jaws, or greater weapon specialization, if you already have weapon specialization with these unarmed attacks.

entries.with: You gain a +4 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d8} slashing damage and has the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack that deals {@damage 1d10} piercing damage. You also gain {@classFeature weapon specialization|Fighter||7} with the claws and jaws, or {@classFeature greater weapon specialization|Fighter||15}, if you already have weapon specialization with these unarmed attacks. The duration is 1 hour.

entries.replace: You gain an item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack with the {@trait agile} trait and a jaws unarmed attack.

raw.name: Biting Snare
entries: The snare's jaws shut on the leg of a creature that steps on it. The snare deals {@damage 5d6} piercing damage to the first creature that enters its square; that creature must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save.

entries.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed until it recovers at least 1 Hit Point.

raw.name: Black Adder Venom
entries: Adder venom is a simple but effective way to enhance a weapon.

raw.name: Black Lotus Extract
entries: Black lotus extract causes severe internal bleeding.

raw.name: Bleeding Spines Snare
entries: When a creature enters the square, spines covered in numerous jagged thorns protrude out to stab it, dealing {@damage 8d8} piercing damage and {@damage 2d8} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage}. The creature must attempt a DC 31 basic Reflex saving throw. After the initial trigger, the spines retract and protrude again repeatedly for 1 minute, forcing any creature that enters the space or ends its turn in the space to attempt a Reflex save against the spines' damage.

raw.name: Blightburn Resin
entries: This tacky, hardened sap is harvested from trees infected by fungal blights and exposed to open flames.

raw.name: Bloodletting Kukri
entries: This +1 striking kukri has a crimson blade that shimmers eerily in bright light. On a critical hit, the kukri deals {@damage 1d8} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage}. If the target didn't already have {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} when you scored the critical hit, you also gain {@dice 1d8} temporary Hit Points.

raw.name: Bloodseeker Beak

activate.trigger: You hit a {@condition flat-footed} creature with the affixed weapon.

entries: This long, hollow proboscis is harvested from the notorious bloodseeker beast and drips a trickle of blood. When you activate the beak, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} precision damage on your damage roll. If you deal sneak attack damage to the creature, you also deal {@damage 1d4} {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage}.

raw.name: Blowgun Dart
entries: These thin, light darts are typically made of hardwood and stabilized with fletching of down or fur. They are often hollow so they can be used to deliver poison.

raw.name: Bolt
entries: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.
{@note Different weapons have different prices for ammunition. The prices listed are thus, only for reference in the weapon statblocks themselves.}

raw.name: Bomb Snare
entries: You create a snare that catalyzes three 3rd-level moderate alchemical bombs of the same type to explode when a creature enters the snare's square. The target and all creatures in adjacent squares must attempt a DC 26 Reflex save, as the snare deals damage equal to three times the direct hit damage from one of the component bombs (for example, {@damage 6d6} electricity damage from bottled lightning) with no {@trait splash} damage or other effects.

entries.Success: The creature takes half damage and no other effects.
entries.Failure: The creature takes full damage. It also takes all other effects of a direct hit from one of the component bombs (such as {@condition flat-footed} from bottled lightning or {@condition persistent damage} from an acid flask).
entries.Critical Failure: The creature takes double damage, plus all other effects of a direct hit (as failure).

craftReq: Supply six of the same damaging 3rd-level moderate alchemical bomb.

raw.name: Bomber's Eye Elixir
entries: This tincture lets you pinpoint your foes. For the next 5 minutes, your alchemical bomb {@action Strike||Strikes} reduce the circumstance bonus to AC your targets gain from cover.
Reduce your targets' circumstance bonus by 1.

entries.with: your targets gain from cover by 1

entries: Reduce your targets' circumstance bonus by 2.

entries.with: your targets gain from cover by 2

raw.name: Boots of Bounding
entries: The springy soles of these sturdy leather boots cushion your feet and make each step lighter. These boots give you a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed and a +2 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks to {@action High Jump} and {@action Long Jump}. In addition, when you use the {@action Leap} action, you can move 5 feet further if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.
The bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the bonus to {@action High Jump} and {@action Long Jump} is +3.

raw.name: Boots of Elvenkind
entries: These tall, pointed boots are made from soft, supple black or green leather and are decorated with trim and buckles of gold. When worn, the boots allow you to move more nimbly, granting you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks.

Until the end of your turn, you ignore {@quickref difficult terrain||3|terrain} when moving on the ground. If you're wearing a cloak of elvenkind, you also gain a +5-foot status bonus to your land Speed until the end of your turn.
The boots grant a +2 bonus. If you're also wearing a cloak of elvenkind, greater boots of elvenkind constantly grant the effects of pass without trace (DC 30) in forest environments.

raw.name: Boots of Speed
entries: These sleek red boots make your legs feel like they're bursting with energy. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your land Speed and to any climb or swim Speeds you have.

You click the heels of the boots together and gain the {@condition quickened} condition for 1 minute. You can use the extra action to {@action Stride}, {@action Climb}, or {@action Swim}. (You must still attempt an {@skill Athletics} check for the {@action Climb} and {@action Swim} actions unless you have the appropriate movement type.)

raw.name: Bottled Air
entries: Appearing to be an ordinary corked glass bottle, this item contains a limitless supply of fresh air. You must uncork the bottle with an {@action Interact} action before you can activate it.

You draw a breath of air from the bottle. This allows you to breathe even in an airless or toxic environment. Air doesn't escape the mouth of the bottle, so leaving the open bottle in an airless environment doesn't change the environment.

raw.name: Bottled Lightning
entries: Bottled lightning is packed with volatile reagents that create a blast of electricity when they are exposed to air. Bottled lightning deals the listed electricity damage and electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.
It deals {@damage 1d6} electricity damage and 1 electricity {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: The bomb deals {@damage 1d6} electricity damage and 1 electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn.

entries: You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 2d6} electricity damage and 2 electricity {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: The bomb deals {@damage 2d6} electricity damage and 2 electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn. You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries: You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 3d6} electricity damage and 3 electricity {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: The bomb deals {@damage 3d6} electricity damage and 3 electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn. You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries: You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals {@damage 4d6} electricity damage and 4 electricity {@trait splash} damage.

entries.with: The bomb deals {@damage 4d6} electricity damage and 4 electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn. You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls.

entries.replace: Bottled lightning deals the listed electricity damage and electricity {@trait splash} damage. On a hit, the target becomes {@condition flat-footed} until the start of your next turn. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

raw.name: Bracelet of Dashing
entries: This jangling, silvery bracelet makes you lighter on your feet, giving you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks.
You gain a +10-foot status bonus to Speed for 1 minute.

raw.name: Bracers of Armor
entries: These stiff leather armguards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5. You can affix talismans to bracers of armor as though they were light armor.

variants.name: Bracers of Armor (Type II)
entries: The item bonus to AC and saves is +2.

variants.name: Bracers of Armor (Type III)
entries: The item bonus to AC and saves is +3.

raw.name: Bracers of Missile Deflection
entries: These bracers are made from plates of durable mithral and gleam like the summer sun.

entries.trigger: A ranged weapon attack hits you but doesn't critically hit
entries.requirements: You are aware of the attack and not {@condition flat-footed}
entries: The bracers send the missile off-course. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If this would cause the attack to be a failure, the attack misses you.
You can activate the bracers once every 10 minutes.

entries.with: once every 10 minutes

raw.name: Bravo's Brew
entries: This flask of foaming beer grants courage. For the next hour after drinking this elixir, you gain an item bonus to Will saves, which is greater when attempting Will saves against {@trait fear}.
The bonus on Will saves is +1, or +2 against {@trait fear}.

entries.with: a +1 item bonus to Will saves, or +2 when attempting Will saves against {@trait fear}.

entries: The bonus on Will saves is +2, or +3 against {@trait fear}.

entries.with: a +2 item bonus to Will saves, or +3 when attempting Will saves against {@trait fear}.

entries: The bonus on Will saves is +3, or +4 against {@trait fear}. If you roll a success on a save against fear, you get a critical success instead.

entries.with: a +3 item bonus to Will saves, or +4 when attempting Will saves against {@trait fear}. If you roll a success on a save against {@trait fear}, you get a critical success instead.

entries.replace: an item bonus to Will saves, which is greater when attempting Will saves against {@trait fear}.

raw.name: Breastplate of Command
entries: This +1 resilient breastplate is made from shining bronze overlaid with reinforcing golden panels in the shape of lion's heads. Wearing this breastplate grants you a commanding aura. You gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Diplomacy} checks, but you take a –2 item penalty to {@skill Stealth} checks to {@action Hide} and {@action Sneak} and {@skill Deception} checks to {@action Impersonate}.

You grant allies within 100 feet a +2 status bonus to saves against {@trait fear} effects for 1 minute. When you activate this ability, each affected ally who's {@condition frightened} reduces their {@condition frightened} value by 1.
The armor is a +2 greater resilient breastplate. The item bonus and penalty increase to +3 and –3, respectively.

raw.name: Brimstone Fumes
entries: Fumes from the forges of Hell drain health and strength alike.

raw.name: Bronze Bull Pendant

activate.requirements: You are trained in {@skill Athletics}.
activate.trigger: You attempt an {@skill Athletics} check to {@action Shove}, but you haven't rolled yet

entries: This pendant is forged from grainy steel and depicts a snorting bull's face. The pendant must be attached to the chest area or on a shoulder guard. When you activate the pendant, you gain a +2 status bonus to the {@skill Athletics} check to {@action Shove}, and if you roll a critical failure on the check, you get a failure instead.

raw.name: Brooch of Shielding
entries: This piece of silver or gold jewelry is adorned with miniature images of kite shields and can be used to fasten a cloak or cape. The brooch automatically absorbs {@spell magic missile} spells targeting you. A brooch of shielding can absorb 30 individual {@spell magic missile}s before it melts and becomes useless.
Sometimes when found, a brooch of shielding has already absorbed a number of missiles.

raw.name: Broom of Flying
entries: This broom has a tenuous connection to gravity, and it tends to drift even while stowed. You can ride on the broom using one hand to guide it, and the broom can carry up to one passenger in addition to you. The broom moves at a fly Speed of 20 feet.
The broom can carry only so much, taking a –10-foot penalty to its Speed if laden with more than 20 Bulk, and crashing to the ground if it carries more than 30 Bulk.

You name a destination on the same plane, and the broom speeds toward it at a fly Speed of 40 feet. You must either clutch the broom with two hands in order to ride it, or you need to release the broom to send it off with no rider. If you don't have a good idea of the location, layout, and general direction of the destination, or if your named destination is on another plane, the broom wanders aimlessly, circling back to its starting location after 30 minutes.
If the broom carries a rider, this activation lasts until 4 hours pass (typically 16 miles of travel), the broom reaches its destination, or you {@action Dismiss} the activation.
If the broom doesn't have a rider, the activation lasts until the broom reaches its destination. When the activation ends, the broom floats to the ground and can't be activated again for 1 hour.

raw.name: Caltrop Snare
entries: This snare consists of a {@condition hidden} canister of {@item caltrops} attached to a trip wire. When the snare is triggered, it flings the caltrops into either the snare's square or a square adjacent to the snare. You choose which square when you set up the snare.
If the {@item caltrops} scatter into the same square as a creature, that creature must attempt the {@skill Acrobatics} check immediately.
craftReq: Supply a container of caltrops.

raw.name: Caltrops
entries: These four-pronged metal spikes can damage a creature's feet. You can scatter caltrops in an empty square adjacent to you with an {@action Interact} action. The first creature that moves into that square must succeed at a DC 14 {@skill Acrobatics} check or take 1d4 piercing damage and 1 {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage}. A creature taking {@condition persistent damage||persistent bleed damage} from caltrops takes a –5-foot penalty to its Speed. Spending an {@action Interact} action to pluck the caltrops free reduces the DC to stop the bleeding. Once a creature takes damage from caltrops, enough are ruined that other creatures moving into the square are safe. Deployed caltrops can be salvaged and reused if no creatures took damage from them. Otherwise, enough are ruined that they can't be salvaged.

raw.name: Candle (10)
entries: A lit candle sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius for 8 hours.

raw.name: Candle of Truth
entries: This tapered candle has a golden wick that burns with white fire.
You activate the candle by lighting it, which causes creatures within 10 feet of the candle to find it difficult to tell falsehoods.
Creatures in the area receive a –4 status penalty to {@action Lie}.

In addition, when first entering the affected area, each creature (including you) must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be unable to tell any deliberate lies while within 10 feet of the lit candle. This lasts for as long as the candle is lit. Once lit, the candle burns for 10 minutes, and it cannot be extinguished.

raw.name: Cape of the Mountebank
entries: This bright red-and-gold cape is often interlaced with glittery threads and serves as a distraction. While wearing the cape, you gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Deception} checks.

You cast {@spell dimension door}. The space you leave and the one you appear in are filled with puffs of smoke that make anyone within {@condition concealed} until they leave the smoke or the end of your next turn, at which point the smoke dissipates.
Strong winds immediately disperse the smoke.

raw.name: Cassock of Devotion
entries: Each cassock of devotion depicts scenes related to the domains of a certain deity. It serves as a religious symbol of that deity, and it doesn't need to be wielded to provide that benefit. You gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Religion} checks and a +1 item bonus to the divine skill of the deity to whom the cassock is dedicated (as listed on pages 437–440).

You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a cleric domain spell for a domain belonging to the deity the cassock is dedicated to. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

craftReq: You are a cleric who worships the deity tied to the cassock.

raw.name: Cat's Eye Elixir
entries: After you consume this elixir, your vision sharpens and you become sensitive to even the most minute movements. For the next minute, you reduce the flat check to target {@condition hidden} creatures to 5, and you don't need to attempt a flat check to target {@condition concealed} creatures. These benefits apply only against creatures within 30 feet of you.

raw.name: Caterwaul Sling
entries: Made of shiny brown leather, this +1 striking sling has a single white thread interwoven into its cord.
You pull the white thread free, then whirl the sling in circles at high speed. It lets out an ear-piercing wave of sound.
Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes {@damage 4d6} sonic damage (DC 21 basic Fortitude save). Any creature that fails is {@condition deafened} for 1 round, or 1 hour on a critical failure.

raw.name: Celestial Armor
entries: This suit of +2 resilient chain mail is made of fine white links of a strange and slightly translucent pale metal, and the sleeves and skirt are fashioned into smaller trails that resemble feathers. Unlike normal chain mail, celestial armor has no Speed reduction, its armor check penalty is 0, and its Bulk is 1.

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against fiends. You appear radiant while you wear the armor, giving you a +2 item bonus to {@skill Diplomacy} checks against all creatures except fiends.
If you are not good, you are {@condition drained 2} while wearing celestial armor. You can't recover from this condition while wearing the armor.
The armor sprouts glowing wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet. The wings shed bright light in a 40-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet). The wings fade away after 10 minutes.

craftReq: You are good.

raw.name: Channel Protection Amulet
entries: This nugget of polished tektite is trapped in a cage of braided wire and hangs from a silken cord. When wearing this amulet, you gain resistance 5 against damage from {@spell harm} spells if you're living, or against {@spell heal} spells if you're undead.

raw.name: Cheetah's Elixir
entries: Enzymatic compounds in this elixir strengthen and excite the muscles in your legs. You gain a status bonus to your Speed for the listed duration.
The bonus is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

entries.with: You gain a +5 status bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

entries: The bonus is +10 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

entries.with: You gain a +10 status bonus to your Speed for 10 minutes.

entries: The bonus is +10 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

entries.with: You gain a +10 status bonus to your Speed for 1 hour.

entries.replace: You gain a status bonus to your Speed for the listed duration.

raw.name: Chest
entries: A wooden chest can hold up to 8 Bulk of items.

raw.name: Chime of Opening
entries: This hollow mithral tube is about a foot long and bears engravings reminiscent of open locks and {@condition broken} chains.
The chime can be activated 10 times before it cracks and becomes useless.

You aim the chime at a container, door, or lock you want to open and strike the chime. The chime sends out magical vibrations that attempt a {@skill Thievery} check against the lock's DC, with a {@skill Thievery} bonus of +13. This targets only one lock or binding at a time, so you might need to activate the chime multiple times to open a target with several forms of protection.

raw.name: Choker of Elocution
entries: This platinum choker bears characters from a language's alphabet, and it gives knowledge of that language and the associated culture's customs. You gain a +1 item bonus to {@skill Society} checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the chosen language.
Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the flat check to perform an auditory action while {@condition deafened} from 5 to 3.

craftReq: You know the language or languages the choker grants.
entries: The item bonus is +2. The choker bears characters from three languages and grants fluency in all three.

raw.name: Circlet of Persuasion
entries: This elegant silver band often resembles curling fig leaves and fits around your brow. You gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Deception} and {@skill Diplomacy} checks.
When you invest the circlet, you either increase your Charisma score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.
You cast a 4th-level {@spell charm} spell (DC 38).

raw.name: Clandestine Cloak
entries: When you pull up the hood of this nondescript gray cloak (an {@action Interact} action), you become drab and uninteresting, gaining a +1 item bonus to {@skill Stealth} checks and to {@skill Deception} checks to {@action Impersonate} a forgettable background character, such as a servant, but also taking a –1 item penalty to {@skill Diplomacy} and {@skill Intimidation} checks.
You pull the cloak's hood up and gain the benefits of nondetection for 1 hour or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first.
The item bonus is +2, and when you activate the cloak, you gain the benefits of 5th-level {@spell nondetection} for 8 hours.

raw.name: Climbing Bolt
entries: The shaft of this bolt is wrapped with fine twine. When the bolt strikes a solid surface, the twine unwinds and enlarges into a 50-foot-long rope, securely fastened to the surface the bolt struck. The rope can be pulled free with an {@action Interact} action and a successful DC 20 {@skill Athletics} check.

raw.name: Climbing Kit
entries: This satchel includes 50 feet of rope, pulleys, a dozen pitons, a hammer, a grappling hook, and one set of crampons. Climbing kits allow you to attach yourself to the wall you're {@action Climb||Climbing}, moving half as quickly as usual (minimum 5 feet) but letting you attempt a DC {@flatDC 5} flat check whenever you critically fail to prevent a fall. A single kit has only enough materials for one climber; each climber needs their own kit. If you wear your climbing kit, you can access it as part of a {@action Climb} action.

variants.name: Extreme Climbing Kit
entries: You gain a +1 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks to {@action Climb} while using an extreme climbing kit.

raw.name: Cloak of Elvenkind
entries: This cloak is deep green with a voluminous hood, embroidered with gold trim and symbols significant in elven culture. The cloak allows you to cast the {@spell ghost sound} cantrip as an arcane innate spell. When you adjust the cloak's clasp (an {@action Interact} action), the cloak transforms to match the environment around you and muffles your sounds, granting you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Stealth} checks.

You draw the hood up and gain the effects of invisibility, with the spell's normal duration or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first. If you're also wearing boots of elvenkind, you can activate this ability twice per day.

The cloak grants a +2 item bonus, and the effects of 4th-level {@spell invisibility}. If you're also wearing boots of elvenkind, the greater cloak of elvenkind allows you to {@action Sneak} in forest environments even when creatures are currently observing you.

raw.name: Cloak of the Bat
entries: Sewn from several long strips of luxurious brown and black silk, this cloak grants you a +2 item bonus to {@skill Stealth} checks as well as to {@skill Acrobatics} checks to {@action Maneuver in Flight}. You can also use your feet to hang from any surface that can support your weight, without requiring any check, though you still must attempt {@skill Athletics} checks to {@action Climb} in order to move around while inverted.

You can either transform the cloak into bat-like wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet for 10 minutes, or have the cloak turn you into a bat by casting a 4th-level {@spell pest form} spell on you.
The item bonus is +3, and you can activate the cloak any number of times per day.
Ordinary clothing is functional with basic tailoring, such as peasant garb, monk's robes, or work clothes.

Explorer's clothing is sturdy enough that it can be reinforced to protect you, even though it isn't a suit of armor. It comes in many forms, though the most common sorts look like {@class cleric||clerical} vestments, {@class monk}'s garments, or {@class wizard}'s robes, as members of all three classes are likely to avoid wearing armor.

Fine clothing, suitable for a noble or royal, is made with expensive fabrics, precious metals, and intricate patterns. You can {@quickref wear||1|wearing tools} only one tool set of light Bulk with fine clothing, instead of the normal limit of 2 Bulk.
You gain a +1 item bonus to checks to {@action Make an Impression} on upper-class folk while wearing high-fashion fine clothing.
Winter clothing allows you to negate the damage from severe environmental cold and reduce the damage from extreme cold to that of severe cold.

raw.name: Cognitive Mutagen
entries: Your mind becomes clear and cognition flows freely, but physical matters seem ephemeral.

{@b Benefit} You gain an item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Crafting}, {@skill Lore}, {@skill Occultism}, and {@skill Society} checks and all checks to {@action Recall Knowledge}. Your critical failures on {@action Recall Knowledge} checks become failures instead.

{@b Drawback} You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, {@skill Athletics} checks, and {@skill Acrobatics} checks. You can carry 2 less Bulk than normal before becoming {@condition encumbered}, and the maximum Bulk you can carry is reduced by 4.
The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

entries.with: You gain a +1 item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Crafting}, {@skill Lore}, {@skill Occultism}, and {@skill Society} checks and all checks to {@action Recall Knowledge} for 1 minute.

entries: The bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

entries.with: You gain a +2 item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Crafting}, {@skill Lore}, {@skill Occultism}, and {@skill Society} checks and all checks to {@action Recall Knowledge} for 10 minutes.

entries: The bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour. You become trained in one Intelligence-based skill, chosen at creation.

entries.with: You gain a +3 item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Crafting}, {@skill Lore}, {@skill Occultism}, and {@skill Society} checks and all checks to {@action Recall Knowledge} for 1 hour. You become trained in one Intelligence-based skill, chosen at creation.

entries: The bonus is +4, and the duration is 1 hour. You become trained in one skill, chosen at creation.

entries.with: You gain a +4 item bonus to {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Crafting}, {@skill Lore}, {@skill Occultism}, and {@skill Society} checks and all checks to {@action Recall Knowledge} for 1 hour. You become trained in one skill, chosen at creation.

entries.replace: You gain an item bonus to \{@skill Arcana\}, \{@skill Crafting\}, \{@skill Lore\}, \{@skill Occultism\}, and \{@skill Society\} checks and all checks to \{@action Recall Knowledge\}.

raw.name: Cold Iron
entries: Weapons made from cold iron are deadly to demons and fey alike.
Cold iron looks like normal iron but is mined from particularly pure sources and shaped with little or no heat. This process is extremely difficult, especially for high-grade cold iron items.

raw.name: Cold Iron Armor
entries: Cold iron armor sickens certain creatures that touch it. A creature with weakness to cold iron (such as most demons and fey) that critically fails an unarmed attack against a creature in cold iron armor becomes {@condition sickened 1}. A creature with weakness to cold iron is {@condition sickened 1} as long as it wears cold iron armor.

craftReq: The initial raw materials must include cold iron worth at least 70 sp + 7 sp per Bulk.
The initial raw materials must include at least 150 gp of cold iron + 15 gp per Bulk.
The initial raw materials must include cold iron worth at least 10,000 gp + 1,000 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Cold Iron Shield
entries: Cold iron shields don't typically have an additional effect, though when used for a shield bash, they are cold iron weapons.

variants.name: Low-Grade Cold Iron Buckler
entries: {@action Craft} Bulk L; Requirements cold iron worth at least 15 sp The shield has Hardness 3, HP 12, and BT 6.
{@action Craft} Bulk 1; Requirements cold iron worth at least 17 sp The shield has Hardness 5, HP 20, and BT 10.

variants.name: Standard-Grade Cold Iron Buckler
craftReq: cold iron worth at least 375 sp The shield has Hardness 5, HP 20, and BT 10.
cold iron worth at least 425 sp The shield has Hardness 7, HP 28, and BT 14.

variants.name: High-Grade Cold Iron Buckler
craftReq: cold iron worth at least 2,500 gp The shield has Hardness 8, HP 32, and BT 16.
cold iron worth at least 2,750 gp The shield has Hardness 10, HP 40, and BT 20.

raw.name: Cold Iron Weapon
entries: Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

craftReq: at least 20 sp of cold iron + 2 sp per Bulk.
at least 110 gp of cold iron + 11 gp per Bulk.
at least 4,500 gp of cold iron + 450 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Collar of Empathy
entries: This ornate collar of intertwined leather strips of contrasting colors is paired with a bracelet of a similar construction.

When you wear and invest the bracelet and your companion wears and invests the collar, you gain a stronger connection to each other. You and your companion can always sense each others' emotional states and basic physical wants and needs.

You perceive through your animal companion's senses instead of your own. You can Sustain the Activation. You are unaware of your own surroundings for as long as you are using your animal companion's senses.

In addition to the obvious use when you are separated from your companion, this ability might allow you to notice sounds, scents, and other stimuli that your companion's senses register but yours alone don't.

raw.name: Collar of Inconspicuousness
entries: This leather collar's worn and almost threadbare look belies its magical nature. When your companion wears and invests the collar, it gains the ability to change its appearance from that of a ferocious animal into a more inconspicuous form.

You touch your animal companion to transform it into a nonthreatening Tiny creature of the same family or a similar creature (for instance, a house cat instead of a tiger, or a puppy instead of a wolf). This has the effects of pest form (2nd level, or 4th level if your companion can fly). The effect lasts until you {@action Dismiss} it.

raw.name: Compass
entries: A compass helps you {@action Sense Direction} or navigate, provided you're in a location with uniform magnetic fields. Without a compass, you take a –2 item penalty to these checks (similar to using a shoddy item).

variants.name: Lensatic Compass
entries: A lensatic compass gives you a +1 item bonus to these checks.

raw.name: Comprehension Elixir
entries: This bitter draught opens your mind to the potential of the written word. For the listed duration after drinking this elixir, you can understand any words you read, so long as they are written in a common language. This elixir doesn't automatically allow you to understand codes or extremely esoteric passages—you still need to attempt a skill check to {@action Decipher Writing}.
The duration is 1 minute.

entries.with: 1 minute

entries: The duration is 10 minutes.

entries.with: 10 minutes

raw.name: Corrosive
entries: Acid sizzles across the surface of the weapon. When you hit with the weapon, add {@damage 1d6} acid damage to the damage dealt.
In addition, on a critical hit, the target's armor (if any) takes {@damage 3d6} acid damage (before applying Hardness); if the target has a shield raised, the shield takes this damage instead.
The acid damage dealt by this weapon ignores the target's acid resistance. Increase the acid damage dealt to armor or a shield on a critical hit to {@dice 6d6}.

raw.name: Coyote Cloak
entries: This dusty coat is made of mangy brown-and-gray coyote fur.
You gain a +1 item bonus to {@skill Survival} checks. If you critically succeed at your {@skill Survival} check to {@action Subsist}, you can feed twice as many additional creatures.
The cloak grants a +2 item bonus, and if you critically succeed at a {@skill Survival} check to {@action Subsist}, you can feed four times as many additional creatures.

raw.name: Crafter's Eyepiece
entries: This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. While worn, this eyepiece gives you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Crafting} checks. When you {@action Repair} an item, increase the Hit Points restored by 15 per proficiency rank instead of 10.
The eyepiece grants a +2 item bonus and can be activated.
You calibrate the eyepiece to have it cast a 5th-level {@spell creation} spell over the course of 1 minute to construct a temporary item.
You calibrate the eyepiece to have it cast a 5th-level {@spell creation} spell over the course of 1 minute to construct a temporary item.

raw.name: Crowbar
entries: When {@action Force Open||Forcing Open} an object that doesn't have an easy grip, a crowbar makes it easier to gain the necessary leverage. Without a crowbar, prying something open takes a –2 item penalty to the {@skill Athletics} check to {@action Force Open}.

variants.name: Levered Crowbar
entries: A levered crowbar grants you a +1 item bonus to {@skill Athletics} checks to {@action Force Open} anything that can be pried open.

raw.name: Crying Angel Pendant

activate.requirements: You are trained in {@skill Medicine}.
activate.trigger: You critically fail to {@action Administer First Aid}

entries: When you activate this alabaster pendant, your critical failure becomes a normal failure instead.

raw.name: Crystal Ball
entries: This polished crystal sphere enhances scrying magic. Any visual information received through a spell with the {@trait scrying} trait that was cast by the crystal ball appears within the sphere, and any auditory information sounds out from the surface of the sphere. When you cast a {@spell scrying} spell by any other means while holding the sphere, you can relay any information you receive in the same way, allowing others to see or hear the target.
The base version of a crystal ball is a sphere of clear quartz, but other versions are made of different stones.
The crystal ball casts clairvoyance to your specifications.
The crystal ball casts a DC 33 {@spell scrying} spell to your specifications.
Scrying is DC 36 and gives you the benefits of {@spell see invisibility} on the target.
Scrying is DC 37 and gives you the benefits of {@spell mind reading} on the target, using the same save DC.
Scrying is DC 39 and gives you the benefits of {@spell telepathy} for communicating with the target.
Scrying is DC 41 and gives you the benefits of {@spell true seeing} for anything you watch through it.

raw.name: Cytillesh Oil
entries: This thick substance is distilled from the mind-robbing cytillesh fungus, though it lacks memory-altering capabilities.

raw.name: Dagger of Venom
entries: The serrated blade of this +1 striking dagger has a greenish tinge, and the hilt is sculpted to look like the head of a serpent about to strike. When you critically succeed at an attack roll with the dagger of venom, the target becomes {@condition sickened 1} unless it succeeds at a DC 19 Fortitude save. This is a {@trait poison} effect. In addition, you can activate the dagger to poison a creature with a more potent poison.
You poison the creature you hit with dagger venom.

raw.name: Dancing
entries: A dancing weapon flies autonomously and strikes your foes.

You {@action Release} the weapon and it dances through the air, fighting on its own against the last enemy you attacked, or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated. At the end of your turn each round, the weapon can {@action Fly} up to its fly Speed of 40 feet, and then can either {@action Fly} again or {@action Strike} one creature within its reach.

The weapon has a space of 5 feet, but it doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide flanking. The weapon can't move through an enemy's space. The weapon can't use reactions, and its {@action Fly} actions don't trigger reactions.

While it's activated, a dancing weapon makes {@action Strike||Strikes} with an attack modifier of +24 plus its item bonus to attack rolls. It uses the weapon's normal damage but has a +0 Strength modifier.
The weapon's abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can't be activated or benefit from any of your abilities while dancing.

Each round, when the weapon is finished using its actions, attempt a DC {@flatDC 6} flat check. On a failure, the activation ends and the weapon falls to the ground. You can't activate the item again for 10 minutes.

raw.name: Dancing Scarf
entries: This long and billowing scarf is typically woven of silk or sheer fabric and adorned with bells or other jangling bits of shiny metal. It grants a +1 item bonus to {@skill Performance} checks to dance.

entries.requirements: On your most recent action, you succeeded at a {@skill Performance} check to dance
entries: You become {@condition concealed} until the beginning of your next turn.
The scarf grants a +2 bonus. When you activate the scarf, you can also {@action Stride} up to half your Speed or {@action Step}.

raw.name: Daredevil Boots
entries: These brightly colored, soft-soled boots motivate you to perform risky stunts and grant you the agility to succeed. The boots grant you a +2 item bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to {@action Tumble Through} an enemy's space.

The boots can grip solid surfaces and help you avoid a fall, allowing you to use the {@action Grab an Edge} reaction even if your hands aren't free. You treat falls as 10 feet shorter or, if you have the Cat Fall feat, treat your proficiency rank in {@skill Acrobatics} as one degree better to determine the benefits of that feat. If you have Cat Fall and are already legendary in {@skill Acrobatics}, you can choose the speed of your fall, from 60 feet per round up to normal falling speed.
The bonus to {@skill Acrobatics} checks is +3, and the bonus to {@action Tumble Through} is +2. The boots can be activated.
{@b Activate} {@as 2} command; {@b Frequency} once per day; {@b Effect} The boots cast {@spell freedom of movement} on you.

raw.name: Darkvision Elixir
entries: After you drink this elixir, your sight becomes sharper in darkness. You gain {@ability darkvision} for the listed duration.
The duration is 10 minutes.

entries.with: 10 minutes

entries: The duration is 1 hour.

entries.with: 1 hour

entries: The duration is 24 hours.

entries.with: 24 hours

raw.name: Darkwood
entries: Darkwood is a very lightweight wood found primarily in oldgrowth forests in south-central Avistan; it is dark as ebony but has a slight purple tint. A darkwood item's Bulk is reduced by 1 (or to light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on an item that normally has light Bulk). The Price of an item made of darkwood is based on the item's normal Bulk, not its reduced Bulk for being made of darkwood, but reduce the Bulk before making any further Bulk adjustments for the size of the item.

raw.name: Darkwood Armor
entries: Darkwood armor is 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on armor that normally has light Bulk). It's easier to wear than normal wood armor, reducing the Strength score necessary to ignore its check penalty by 2 and reducing its Speed penalty by 5 feet. (There are no types of wood armor in this book.)

craftReq: The initial raw materials must include darkwood worth at least 200 gp + 20 gp per Bulk.
The initial raw materials must include darkwood worth at least 16,000 gp + 1,600 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Darkwood Shield
entries: Darkwood shields are 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if their normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on a shield that normally has light Bulk).

variants.name: Standard-Grade Darkwood Buckler
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 50 gp

variants.name: Standard-Grade Darkwood Shield
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 55 gp

variants.name: Standard-Grade Darkwood Tower Shield
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 70 gp

variants.name: High-Grade Darkwood Buckler
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 4,000 gp

variants.name: High-Grade Darkwood Shield
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 4,400 gp

variants.name: High-Grade Darkwood Tower Shield
craftReq: darkwood worth at least 5,600 gp

raw.name: Darkwood Weapon
entries: Darkwood weapons are as dark as ebony, with a slight purple tint. A darkwood weapon's Bulk is reduced by 1 (or to light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on a weapon that normally has light Bulk).

craftReq: at least 175 gp of darkwood + 17.5 gp per Bulk.
at least 6,750 gp of darkwood + 675 gp per Bulk.

raw.name: Dazing Coil

activate.trigger: You deal damage to a {@condition flat-footed} creature with the affixed weapon.

entries: This knot of copper wire reshapes itself in a new pattern every time its affixed weapon deals damage. When you activate the coil, the damaged creature must succeed at a DC 31 Will save or be {@condition stunned 1}. If it critically fails, it instead becomes {@condition stunned 2}.

raw.name: Deathcap Powder
entries: The toxic deathcap mushroom can be dried, ground, and treated to form a flavorless powder with accelerated effects.

raw.name: Decanter of Endless Water
entries: This item looks like an ordinary glass flask full of water.

The stopper can't be removed unless you speak one of the item's three command words, each of which causes water to pour forth in a different way. Pulling the stopper straight out creates fresh water, and rotating it as you pull creates salt water. Any effect of the decanter lasts until the decanter is plugged (with its own stopper, a finger, or the like).
Speaking "stream," you cause water to pour out at a rate of 1 gallon per round.
Speaking "fountain," you cause water to pour out in a 5-foot-long stream at a rate of 5 gallons per round.

Speaking "geyser," you cause a powerful deluge of water to erupt at a rate of 15 gallons per round. You can direct the stream at a creature, subjecting it to the effects of hydraulic push (spell attack roll +15). You can repeat this once per round as long as the geyser continues, spending an {@action Interact} action to direct the geyser each time.

raw.name: Demon Armor
entries: Crafted from black iron, this crude suit of +2 resilient full plate is designed to make you look like a horned demon, with your face peering out of the screaming maw of the beast.

While wearing the armor, you can attack with the helmet's horns. They are a martial melee weapon with the effects of a +2 weapon potency rune. They deal {@damage 2d8} piercing damage and have the {@trait deadly <d12>} trait. On a critical hit with the horns, the target must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save against the Abyssal plague disease (page 316). The horns can't be etched with any runes.
If you aren't evil, you're {@condition drained 2} and can't recover from this condition while wearing demon armor.
You cast {@spell dimension door}.

craftReq: You are evil; supply one casting of dimension door.

raw.name: Demon Mask
entries: This terrifying mask is crafted in the visage of a leering demon and grants a +1 item bonus to {@skill Intimidation} checks.
The mask casts a {@spell fear} spell with a DC of 20.
The mask grants a +2 item bonus. It casts 3rd-level {@spell fear} with a DC of 29.

raw.name: Diadem of Intellect
entries: An elegant, colorful gem cut into a complex geometric pattern is slotted into a narrow metal band that fits around your brow. You gain a +3 item bonus to checks to {@action Recall Knowledge}, regardless of the skill. When you invest the diadem, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score.
